DISCOVERY 612, NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO INTERROGATORIES AND INSPECTION DEMANDS PROPOUNDED TO PLAINTIFF AND FOR ORDER DEEMING ADMITTED THE TRUTH OF THE MATTERS SPECIFIED IN REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS IS MOOT IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. THE MOTION IS MOOT AS TO THE INTERROGATORIES AND INSPECTION DEMANDS AS RESPONSES HAVE BEEN SERVED. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS IT SEEKS AN ORDER DEEMING ADMITTED THE TRUTH OF MATTERS SPECIFIED IN THE REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS AS RESPONSES HAVE BEEN SERVED. THE COURT WILL NOT DETERMINE THE ADEQUACY OF THE RESPONSES ON THIS MOTION. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE LATE RESPONSES WERE A MISUSE OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS. SANCTIONS ARE IMPOSED AGAINST PLAINTIFF IN THE AMOUNT OF $470, TO BE PAID WITHIN 30 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
ORDER GRANTING IN PART AND DENYING IN PART MOTION OF DEFENDANTS AND CROSS COMPLAINANTS TO COMPEL DISCOVERY RESPONSES AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS.
